reasoning: The National Financial Conditions Risk Subindex has been decreasing since early 2025 but has recently flatlined around -0.576 over the past four weeks. The FRED trend analysis provides a baseline exceeding probability of 0.46. Given the strong recent downward momentum that has just started to plateau, a short-term increase is slightly less likely than a continued decline or no change, so the first probability is estimated at 0.44. Over longer horizons, mean reversion out of this very loose condition (-0.576) becomes more likely, pulling the probability upward toward 0.50.
